title: Senior Software Engineer, Custody
description: Design, develop, and maintain mission-critical custody systems across multiple blockchains. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define system architecture. Ensure reliability, security, and scalability through best practices. Optimize infrastructure for performance and latency. Mentor junior engineers. Stay informed on blockchain and distributed systems trends.
requirements: 5+ years software engineering experience in fast-paced environments
Proficiency in Golang or Java (Kotlin, Rust, Python, C++ also valued)
Computer science fundamentals: data structures, algorithms, distributed systems
Cloud-native services: Kubernetes, Docker, Kafka, AWS
Strong debugging and production problem-solving skills
Experience with high-throughput, low-latency systems (strong plus)
Trading infrastructure, cryptography, or custody platforms (strong plus)
notes: APPLIED 4/20. Excellent fit - 92/100 match. Go expertise with high-volume transaction systems (Equifax chargeback platform). Distributed systems and AWS/GCP architecture directly align. Team leadership and production debugging experience match. CRITICAL: Application form field required crypto experience - user does not have this. May be a disqualifying factor. Key gaps: no explicit blockchain/custody experience, limited recent Java, cryptography not mentioned. Tailored resume and cover letter generated.
key_strengths: Go proficiency with high-volume transaction systems (Equifax chargeback platform)
Deep distributed systems and architecture expertise across AWS/GCP
Hands-on team leadership and mentorship (led engineers for 2+ years)
Production system debugging and optimization in mission-critical environments
key_gaps: No explicit blockchain/custody platform experience (though distributed systems foundation is solid)
Limited recent Java exposure (role lists Java as preferred alternative)
Cryptography not explicitly mentioned in work history
NO CRYPTO EXPERIENCE - application form flagged this as required

jobs.title: Senior Backend Engineer - Platform Security
jobs.description: Product-focused systems engineer role on Teleport's Platform Security team. Implements security controls, works with security researchers to find and fix vulnerabilities. Work on full product lifecycle from customer problems to design, implementation, testing, and documentation. Open source project written in Go or Rust.
requirements: Comfortable changing focus and working directly with customers
jobs.benefits: Extensive health coverage, annual expense budget, rest & recovery policies, retirement savings plans, equity in $1.1bn business, professional development
jobs.notes: User applied to this role. Interview process includes 30-min intro call, Slack channel submission, and coding challenge in Go or Rust.

jobs.title: Developer Productivity Engineer
jobs.description: Own the build/test/deploy pipeline for 50+ engineers pushing 75+ commits/day. Improve monorepo productivity (JavaScript/TypeScript with Go and Python). Drive excellence in testing and test coverage. Support multi-region and multi-cloud backend infrastructure. Operational excellence and incident prevention.
requirements: Passion for solving hard technical problems
High ownership and comfortable in fast-paced startup environment
Driven significant improvement in productivity of 50+ person dev team
Strong development fundamentals and framework-level improvements
Comfortable diving into feature code (backend, complicated systems)
key_strengths: Led team improving delivery timelines and reducing deployment friction (2+ years at Equifax)
Owned build/test/deploy infrastructure for high-volume backend services (millions of transactions/month)
Deep experience with Go, AWS, and GCP multi-cloud infrastructure
Designed distributed systems at scale reducing incident frequency
20+ years building and owning backend systems and distributed architectures
Proven ability to improve internal tooling and developer experience
key_gaps: No explicit monorepo optimization experience (though distributed systems background is strong)
Limited recent JavaScript/TypeScript focused work (role requires JS/TS codebase navigation)
No public-facing developer tools or frameworks development shown
Limited evidence of testing infrastructure/test coverage optimization
jobs.notes: Strong fit for role. Ian's experience at Equifax with team leadership, infrastructure ownership, and deployment velocity improvements directly aligns with core job responsibilities. Go expertise and multi-cloud infrastructure experience are valuable. Primary gaps are JS/TS codebase and testing infrastructure focus. Tailored resume and cover letter generated.
